new_value: Workspace scaffolded with harmony-crypto and harmony-identity crates
SHA-256 full/truncated hash + BLAKE3 + name_hash implemented with known-vector tests
HKDF-SHA256 implemented, passes all 3 RFC 5869 test vectors
Fernet AES-256-CBC+HMAC-SHA256 implemented, Reticulum-compatible token format
ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD implemented with full test coverage
Ed25519/X25519 key gen + address derivation + serialization implemented
ECDH key exchange, ephemeral encrypt/decrypt, Ed25519 sign/verify all implemented

old_value: {"id":"harmony-ph1","title":"Cross-verify Harmony identity/crypto with Reticulum test vectors","description":"Generate identities in Reticulum Python, export key bytes and ciphertext. Verify Harmony Rust code produces identical hashes, can decrypt Reticulum-encrypted data, and validates Reticulum signatures. This proves interoperability.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T19:56:25Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-01T19:56:25Z"}

new_value: 8 interop tests pass: address derivation, signature verification, signature determinism, decryption of Reticulum ciphertext (2 identities), bidirectional encrypt/decrypt
Done: harmony-reticulum crate implemented with packet format, destination naming/hashing, announce build/validate. 55 tests + 6 interop tests against Python Reticulum. Merged.
Serialization done: from_private_bytes/to_private_bytes, from_public_bytes/to_public_bytes all implemented in harmony-identity. Encrypted keystore deferred to harmony-node CLI work.

old_value:
{"id":"harmony-4uw","title":"Implement Reticulum Interface trait and basic interface types","description":"Phase 2 continued: The Interface trait is the abstraction between Reticulum's protocol logic and physical/logical transports.

Define:
- Interface trait: send(packet), receive() → packet, mtu(), online(), bitrate()
- InterfaceMode: Full, Gateway, AccessPoint, Roaming
- IFAC (authenticated interface) support: HMAC-based interface authentication
- Basic in-memory interface for testing (loopback)

This is the boundary where sans-I/O meets real I/O. The trait must be transport-agnostic so we can later plug in TCP, UDP, serial, LoRa, Zenoh, etc. Harmony nodes will use this to connect Reticulum control plane traffic across whatever physical links are available.

Key design: the interface reports its bitrate/capacity so the node can make intelligent decisions about bandwidth allocation — prioritizing control plane traffic and being a good neighbor on constrained links.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T22:03:57Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-01T22:03:57Z"}
